title: Passwords should not be stored in plaintext or with a fast hashing algorithm
description: This rule checks if passwords are stored in plaintext or with a fast hashing algorithm.
message: A password should not be stored in plaintext or hashed with a fast algorithm.
recommendation: Use a secure hashing algorithm and store hashed passwords.

noncompliant.description: Reading a password from an environment variable.
Using bcrypt's `gensalt` which uses a fast salt.

compliant.description: Reading a hashed password from an environment variable.
Using bcrypt with a slow salt (12 rounds).

why_is_this_an_issue: Storing passwords in plaintext or with a fast hashing algorithm makes them easily accessible to attackers, increasing the risk of unauthorized access.
how_to_fix: Store passwords securely using a slow hashing algorithm and a salt.

metadata.title: Passwords should not be stored in plaintext or with a fast hashing algorithm
